plan: 1. Create `src/ui/rerun-button.ts` — a factory function `createRerunButton(onRerun: () => Promise<void>): HTMLElement` that creates a `<button class="btn btn-secondary receipt-rerun-btn">Re-run Audit</button>`. The click handler disables the button, swaps text to a loading label (e.g. 'Scanning…'), calls the async `onRerun` callback, then restores the button.

2. Add CSS for the rerun button and loading state in `src/styles/receipt.css`: `.receipt-actions` container (flex, right-aligned, margin-top), `.receipt-rerun-btn--loading` with a subtle pulse/opacity animation, and `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override that disables animation.

3. Refactor `src/modules/fingerprint/fingerprint-receipt.ts` — change `renderFingerprintReceipt()` to: (a) build the receipt as before, (b) create a `.receipt-actions` div, (c) use `createRerunButton()` with a callback that calls `collectSnapshot()`, `formatSnapshotToRows()`, clears and repopulates the `.receipt-rows` container, and (d) append the actions div to the receipt container. Add a short artificial delay (~300ms minimum) so the loading state is perceptible.
4. Add `aria-live="polite"` to the `.receipt-rows` container so screen readers announce updated content after rerun.

5. Create test file `tests/us-017/rerun-button.test.ts` — unit tests for `createRerunButton`: renders a button element, button has correct class and text, clicking triggers the callback, button is disabled during callback execution, text changes to loading label during callback, button re-enables after callback resolves.

6. Create test file `tests/us-017/fingerprint-rerun.test.ts` — integration tests for the rerun flow: receipt contains a rerun button, clicking rerun updates `.receipt-rows` children, loading state is shown during rerun (button disabled + loading class), receipt rows reflect fresh snapshot data after rerun, aria-live attribute is present on rows container.
7. Run `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test` to validate all acceptance criteria pass.
testing_recommendations: Unit test `createRerunButton`: verify DOM output (tag, classes, text), verify click calls callback, verify disabled state during async callback, verify text reverts after callback resolves, verify button re-enables on callback rejection (error path).
Integration test `renderFingerprintReceipt` rerun flow: mount receipt, click rerun button, assert `.receipt-rows` children are replaced with fresh data, assert loading class appears and disappears.
Accessibility test: verify `aria-live="polite"` on rows container, verify button has accessible name, verify focus is not lost after rerun completes.
Verify `prefers-reduced-motion` media query disables loading animation (manual or Playwright emulation).
Playwright smoke test: load app, wait for receipt, click Re-run Audit, verify receipt updates without page reload and no console errors.
risks: Signal collection is synchronous and near-instant, so the loading state may flash too quickly to be visible — mitigate with a minimum display duration (~300ms).
Frozen `Object.freeze()` snapshot objects are immutable, but since `collectSnapshot()` returns a new object each call this is not an issue — just ensure we never try to mutate an existing snapshot.

DOM replacement of `.receipt-rows` children could cause focus loss if the rerun button is inside the replaced area — keep the button outside `.receipt-rows` in a separate `.receipt-actions` container.
Existing tests assert exactly 12 `.receipt-row` elements — adding the button outside `.receipt-rows` avoids breaking those selectors, but integration tests should be verified.
